notes: all combat numbers are simulator output on unverified constants
checkpoint levels are provisional (no level timeline in the match feed)
evidence deltas carry finished-inventory survivorship bias

this build stacks over +100% attack speed; the sim now caps attacks/sec at the stated 3.0 (Cursed Ring tooltip), so sustained-DPS is no longer uncapped — the exact cap interaction still merits a practice-mode check
the sim opens on Sky Splitter, which the field has tried and lost with (-0.2% winrate over 68 games) — treat this opener as a sim blind-spot and weight the field

coachLine: Sim-optimal for sustained DPS: first spike lands around minute 12 (Sky Splitter), third item by minute 24. Take Demiurge: +17.3% on this kit’s main output at minute 15.

all.name: Marrow
all.note: Marrow (Major): missing-health-dependent omnivamp; no sustain-uptime model yet

all.name: Nihil
all.note: Nihil (Major): accrual rate depends on combat uptime (how long Doom persists per fight), which the closed-form sim cannot observe

all.name: Aion
all.note: Aion (Major): economy effect; valuable but outside combat objectives (revisit with gold curves)

all.name: Exarch
all.note: Exarch (Major): buffs the HEAL TARGET (usually an ally) — team-side value outside the single-hero sim; self-heal uptime depends on heal cadence vs the 4s window

metaBuilds.title: Crit Skirmisher
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for 10s rotation damage, online around minute 24 — about 1,122 damage over a 10s rotation in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +10% more 10s rotation damage: try Sky Splitter over Solaris (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Crit Drain
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for lifesteal sustain, online around minute 24 — about 145 HP drained back over a 10s fight in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +48% more lifesteal sustain: try Sky Splitter over Vanquisher (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Crit DPS Carry
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for sustained auto DPS, online around minute 25 — about 156 auto-attack DPS in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +91% more sustained auto DPS: try Sky Splitter over Vanquisher (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

matchups.gameplan: Your window is early: force trades before their core completes, and do not coin-flip fights past the crossover. Sparrow ramps on auto-attacks — pressure their positioning before they itemize, and armor blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Deathstalker: their 3s all-in loses 36% of its bite; you give up 29% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Murdock deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Deathstalker: their 3s all-in loses 25% of its bite; you give up 29% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Revenant ramps on auto-attacks — pressure their positioning before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Deathstalker: their 3s all-in loses 25% of its bite; you give up 29% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Drongo deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Deathstalker: their 3s all-in loses 25% of its bite; you give up 29% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Your window is early: force trades before their core completes, and do not coin-flip fights past the crossover. Skylar ramps on auto-attacks — pressure their positioning before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Deathstalker: their 3s all-in loses 25% of its bite; you give up 29% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Your kill threat peaks early-to-mid: cash it in before their later items level the exchange. TwinBlast ramps on auto-attacks — pressure their positioning before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Deathstalker: their 3s all-in loses 25% of its bite; you give up 29% of your damage.

laneSteer.provenance: “Focal Lens” ⇒ ability-burst; the sim models this augment — field carry 48.7% over 1,010 games.
